commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Stars, or angels, who are compared to the stars, and often fought for Israel, 2 Machabees x. (Vales, Philos. ) (Calmet) The favourable and malignant influences of the stars, which the Rabbins talk of, would here be nugatory, (Haydock) unless they might contribute to bring on rain. (Cajetan) Josephus ( v. ) informs us that a furious tempest of hail, met the enemy in the face, and rendered all their efforts useless. (Calmet) A similar instance of the divine protection was obtained by the prayers of the thundering legion, in the army of M. Aurelius; (Tertullian; Eusebius, Hist. ) and again, when Theodosius attacked the tyrant Eugenius, of which Claudian speaks, (in 3 Cons. ) "Te propter gelid is Aquilo de monte procellis Obruit adversas acies, revolutaque tela Vertit in Auctores et turbine repulit hastas O nimium dilecte Deo, cui fundit ab antris Æolus armatas hiemes, cui militat æther Et conjurati veniunt ad classica venti. "(Haydock) Courses. This miracle was of a different kind from that which proved so fatal to the enemies of Josue. (Lyranus) Septuagint Alexandrian, "They fought with (meta) Israel "for which Grabe puts, against Sisara.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
